schema.title: Response Receive V1 Callbacks  Psp   Merchant Id  Post

get.description:
Disclose demo-mode metadata to the login page.

Returns 204 on installs that aren't in demo mode so the SPA's
fetch-on-mount silently bypasses the banner. Returns the bare
`enabled` flag + a notice on demo installs — no credentials, ever.
The legacy PAYHUB_DEMO_USERNAME/PASSWORD env vars are honoured for
one release for backward-compat with the live demo box; they will be
removed when the demo branch is rebased onto this change.

schema.title: Response Submit Access Request V1 Demo Access Requests Post
Response Token Login V1 Demo Login Post
Response Login Mfa Admin Login Mfa Post

post.description:
Generate a fresh set of recovery codes. Wipes any prior set —
the new list is the only valid one from now on.

schema.title: Response List Merchants Admin Merchants Get

delete.description:
Hard-delete a merchant and its dependent configuration.

Allowed only when the merchant has zero payments — the entity that
represents customer money movement. Operator-managed configuration
(api keys, gateway configs, webhook endpoints, settlement files +
rows, idempotency keys) is cascaded inside the same transaction so
the FK RESTRICTs don't fire and so deletion is a single audited
event.

schema.title: Response List Api Keys Admin Merchants  Merchant Id  Api Keys Get

post.description:
Issue a new secret + key_id while preserving the row's metadata
(scopes, allowed_ips, rate_limit_tier). Old key_id becomes revoked
in the same transaction so any cached secret stops working
immediately. Returns the new full credential exactly once.

Rotation is a single-shot replacement; integrations using the old
key need to be updated before this is called. For a grace-window
rotation (both keys live for N hours), create a new key explicitly,
then revoke the old one when ready.

schema.title: Response List Gateways Admin Merchants  Merchant Id  Gateways Get
Response List Endpoints Admin Merchants  Merchant Id  Webhooks Get
Response List Deliveries Admin Deliveries Get

get.description:
Full debugging view of one delivery.

Reconstructs the exact JSON body the worker POSTed (or would POST on
next attempt), the headers, the response excerpt, and links to the
endpoint that owns it. The HMAC signature is *not* exposed because
showing it leaks the secret indirectly; merchants verify with their
own copy of the secret using the byte sequence we display here.

post.description:
Mark many deliveries as resolved in one shot.

Useful when a downstream incident produced a batch of failed
deliveries that the operator handled out-of-band. Skips IDs that
don't exist or are already terminal so a re-run is safe.

Mark a delivery as resolved without retrying — operator says 'we
handled this out of band'.

schema.title: Response List Payments Admin Payments Get

post.description:
Re-poll the PSP for an in-flight payment.

Calls the adapter's `query()` and applies whatever the PSP returns
through the standard transition() chokepoint. No-ops if the payment
is already terminal. 422 if the PSP doesn't support `query()`.

Operator-attested manual transition for stuck payments.

Writes both a `payment_events` row (via transition()) AND an
`audit_log` row — the same double-write record_refund does — so the
forensic trail is reachable from either side.

Mint a time-limited URL pointing at the hosted-checkout page.

Useful when the merchant integration is server-only and needs a URL
to send to a customer over SMS / email. The returned URL is the
`Settings.public_url` joined with the path; in production deployments
that resolves to the WAF host so the merchant doesn't have to
rewrite anything.

schema.title: Response List Files Admin Settlements Get
Response List Rows Admin Settlements  File Id  Rows Get

get.description:
Return the raw bytes of the originally-uploaded settlement file.

Useful when an operator wants to re-ingest with different period
bounds, or hand the file to the PSP for arbitration on a mismatch.
Unlike `/export.csv`, this returns the *exact* uploaded content
byte-for-byte (file_sha256 in metadata can be recomputed against
this body).

Stream filtered settlement rows as a CSV.

Column order matches `services.reconcile.parse_csv` so the export
round-trips through ingest cleanly: piping this back into the upload
endpoint produces the same matched/mismatch classification minus
rows that were classified `missing_in_psp` (they have no PSP-side
representation in the CSV by definition).
Which PSPs publish a programmatic export the puller can use.

schema.title: Response List Supported Sources Admin Settlements Sources Supported Get

post.description:
Operator-triggered ad-hoc settlement pull. Enqueues an Arq job; the
worker fans out across all configured (merchant, psp) pairs (or just
the one passed in `body`) and ingests the results through the same
reconcile pipeline as a manual upload.

schema.title: Response List Audit Admin Audit Get
Response List Admin Users Admin Admin Users Get

delete.description:
Soft-disable an admin user. Status flips to 'disabled' so any
live cookies they hold start failing on the next request (the
`current_admin` dep rejects users whose status isn't 'active'). We
don't hard-delete because `audit_log.actor_id` references this row.

post.description:
Clear failed_login_count + locked_until on an admin user.

Records an audit row tagged actor_type='admin' so the override is
traceable back to the operator who used it.

get.description:
Surface accounts the auto-lockout policy currently has locked,
so the operator can find and unlock them without grepping the DB.

schema.title: Response List Users Admin Merchants  Merchant Id  Users Get

delete.description:
Soft-disable. Sessions get rejected on next request via the
`current_merchant_user` dep's status check.

post.description:
Admin-mediated reset. Returns the one-time token + URL exactly
once. Admin is responsible for sharing it OOB.

Escape hatch when a merchant user lost their authenticator.
Wipes the TOTP secret + flag + backup codes. The user must re-
enrol on next login.

schema.title: Response List Requests Admin Demo Requests Get
Response Approve Admin Demo Requests  Request Id  Approve Post
Response Reject Admin Demo Requests  Request Id  Reject Post
Response Revoke Admin Demo Requests  Request Id  Revoke Post
Response Login Merchant Auth Login Post

post.description:
Second step of MFA-protected login. Pass the `challenge_token`
returned by /login plus the TOTP code; on success the session cookie
is issued.

schema.title: Response Login Mfa Merchant Auth Login Mfa Post

patch.description:
Edit own profile fields. Mobile/email change clears the matching
`*_verified_at` flag — the user must re-verify via the verification
endpoints. Verification messages are dispatched by the notify
service when (and only when) a non-noop provider is configured.

post.description:
Argon2 verify the old password, then re-hash and store the new
one. If the user has TOTP enabled, require a fresh code in the same
request to defeat session-cookie-theft.

Always returns 204 — the response shape never reveals whether
the user exists. When the user does exist and `channel` matches a
contact field on file, we mint a one-shot token and dispatch.

Consume a one-shot token, set the new password. Generic 401 on
every failure path (wrong token, expired, consumed, wrong identity).

Consume an email-verify token. Looks up by hash across all
unverified rows because the token IS the identity here.

Consume a mobile OTP. Auth required because the OTP is short
(6 digits) and brute-forceable without a sessioned anchor.

schema.title: Response List Payments Merchant Payments Get
Response List Keys Merchant Api Keys Get
Response List Endpoints Merchant Webhook Endpoints Get
Response List Files Merchant Settlements Get
Response List Rows Merchant Settlements  File Id  Rows Get
Response List Peers Merchant Users Get

post.description:
Create a new merchant user. A reset token is auto-issued and
sent to the configured contact (email preferred, mobile fallback).
The new user uses that token to set their first password.

Wipe a peer's TOTP. Requires the *owner's* own MFA code in the
same request — defeats stolen-cookie escalation.

schema.title: Response List Gateways Merchant Gateways Get
Response Status Checkout  Payment Id  Status Get

AdminRole.title: AdminRole
AdminRole.description:
Admin user roles. Persisted as plain strings; the DB CHECK constraint
in migration 20260504_0008 enforces membership. The permission matrix in
`app/core/rbac.py` is the single source of truth for what each role can do.
`admin` is the only role available on the Lite license tier; the other
four are unlocked by `Features.rbac_extended_roles` (Pro/Enterprise).

codes.title: Codes
codes.description: Plain-text codes — show once, then never again.

ClearMfaBody.title: ClearMfaBody
ClearMfaBody.description: Owner's own TOTP code; refuses if owner has no MFA themselves.

CreateMerchantUserOut.title: CreateMerchantUserOut
CreateMerchantUserOut.description:
Create-response. If the admin didn't supply a password, the
merchant gets a reset token they share with the user OOB.

target_status.title: Target Status
target_status.description: One of: succeeded, failed, expired. transition() enforces legality.

hosted_checkout.title: Hosted Checkout
hosted_checkout.description: When true, response includes `hosted_checkout_url`: a short-lived URL the customer can be redirected to in order to complete the OTP / redirect / QR flow on a PayHub-hosted page.

MerchantOrgType.title: MerchantOrgType
MerchantOrgType.description:
Merchant org legal form. Set by admin at create-time; merchant
owners can flip via the self-serve org page.

MerchantRole.title: MerchantRole
MerchantRole.description:
Roles within a merchant org. Matrix lives in
`app/core/merchant_rbac.py`. The DB CHECK constraint added in
migration 20260504_0009 pins the column to these values.

`owner` is required: every merchant must always have at least one
active owner (last-owner invariant in the API layer).

MerchantTokenPurpose.title: MerchantTokenPurpose
MerchantTokenPurpose.description:
Single-use token purposes — password reset, email + mobile
verification — multiplexed onto one `merchant_user_tokens` table.

is_full.title: Is Full
is_full.description: True = full refund, transitions payment to 'refunded'. False = partial, logs an event but the payment stays 'succeeded' so further partials are possible.

RecordRefundBody.title: RecordRefundBody
RecordRefundBody.description:
Operator-recorded refund — all Libyan PSPs settle refunds in their
back-office, so the hub never *calls* a refund API. This endpoint is
a tracking step: it records that a refund happened externally so the
state machine moves to `refunded` and reconciliation balances out.

WebhookEndpointPatch.title: WebhookEndpointPatch
WebhookEndpointPatch.description:
Partial update — pass only the fields you want changed.

The HMAC secret is *not* included on purpose: rotating a secret is a
security-sensitive event with merchant-side consequences. Use the
create flow with a new secret and deactivate the old endpoint.

token.title: Token
token.description: The raw token. Show once, then never again.

app__api__admin__merchant_users__IssueRe.title: IssueResetOut
app__api__admin__merchant_users__IssueRe.description: One-shot token returned to the admin for OOB sharing.

app__api__admin__merchants__ApiKeyUpdate.title: ApiKeyUpdate
app__api__admin__merchants__ApiKeyUpdate.description: Partial update for an existing key; secret is never re-issued here.

secret.title: Secret
secret.description: Base32 secret — show as fallback if QR fails

otpauth_uri.title: Otpauth Uri
otpauth_uri.description: Render this as a QR code in the UI

secret.title: Secret
secret.description: The HMAC secret your receiver will check
